name: Timeline generation and artifact routing robustness

setup.description: Four landmark heart failure trials spanning 1999–2022. Tests timeline artifact generation from chronologically ordered evidence, single-paper artifact generation, and queries that should NOT trigger artifact routing. The timeline must respect actual publication dates, not fabricate events, and cite every claim.

papers.title: PARADIGM-HF: Angiotensin-Neprilysin Inhibition versus Enalapril in Heart Failure
DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Heart Failure and Reduced Ejection Fraction
DELIVER: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with a Preserved Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Spironolactone reduced the risk of death from all causes by 30% (RR 0.70; 95% CI 0.60-0.82; P<0.001) among patients with severe heart failure (NYHA class III-IV) and LVEF ≤35%.
1663 patients with NYHA class III-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤35% were randomized to spironolactone 25mg or placebo. The trial was stopped early due to the magnitude of benefit.
Sacubitril/valsartan reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure by 20% compared with enalapril (HR 0.80; 95% CI 0.73-0.87; P<0.001).
8442 patients with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40% were enrolled. The trial was stopped early for overwhelming benefit at a median follow-up of 27 months.
Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.74; 95% CI 0.65-0.85; P<0.001). The number needed to treat was 21 over a median follow-up of 18.2 months.
Enrolled patients were aged ≥18 years with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40%. Patients were randomized 1:1 to dapagliflozin 10mg or placebo.
Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure in patients with heart failure and a preserved ejection fraction (HR 0.79; 95% CI 0.69-0.90; P<0.001).
6263 patients with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF >40% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 2.2 years. Unlike HFrEF trials, DELIVER enrolled patients with preserved ejection fraction.

queries.query: Create a timeline of major heart failure trials from these papers
expectedBehavior: RALES (1999) appears first, DELIVER (2022) appears last
Key results mentioned for each trial
No fabricated dates or events between known trials
failurePatterns: Missing any of the four trials
Fabricates events not in the sources

queries.query: What was the primary result of the RALES trial?
expectedBehavior: NOT routed as an artifact — this is a plain question
Answers with RALES RR 0.70 and P<0.001
Mentions severe heart failure (NYHA III-IV) population
Does NOT generate study guide, FAQ, or timeline structure
failurePatterns: Generates artifact structure for a plain question

qualityCriteria: Timeline entries are in strict chronological order (1999 → 2014 → 2019 → 2022)
Every timeline entry has at least one citation [N]
No fabricated trials, dates, or events not in the sources
Plain question (q2) is NOT routed as an artifact
RR (not HR) used for RALES result since that's what the source says
DELIVER correctly noted as preserved EF trial
